name: SPIRIT 2013
description: Standard Protocol Items: Recommendations for Interventional Trials.

items.title: Title
items.description: Descriptive title identifying the study design, population, interventions, and, if applicable, trial acronym.

items.title: Trial registration
items.description: Trial identifier and registry name; if not yet registered, name of intended registry.

items.title: Protocol version
items.description: Date and version identifier.

items.title: Funding
items.description: Sources and types of financial, material, and other support.

items.title: Roles and responsibilities
items.description: Names, affiliations, and roles of protocol contributors, sponsor and funders, and trial committees.

items.title: Background and rationale
items.description: Description of research question and justification for undertaking the trial, including summary of relevant studies (published and unpublished); explanation for choice of comparators.

items.title: Objectives
items.description: Specific objectives or hypotheses.

items.title: Trial design
items.description: Description of trial design including type of trial (e.g. parallel group, crossover, factorial, single group), allocation ratio, and framework (e.g. superiority, equivalence, non-inferiority, exploratory).

items.title: Study setting
items.description: Description of study settings (e.g. community clinic, academic hospital) and list of countries where data will be collected.

items.title: Eligibility criteria
items.description: Inclusion and exclusion criteria for participants; criteria for individuals performing the interventions if applicable.

items.title: Interventions
items.description: Interventions for each group with sufficient detail to allow replication, including how and when they will be administered; criteria for discontinuing or modifying allocated interventions; strategies to improve adherence; and relevant concomitant care.

items.title: Outcomes
items.description: Primary, secondary, and other outcomes, including the specific measurement variable (e.g. systolic blood pressure), analysis metric, method of aggregation, and time point.

items.title: Participant timeline
items.description: Time schedule of enrolment, interventions, assessments, and visits for participants; a schematic diagram is highly recommended.

items.title: Sample size
items.description: Estimated number of participants needed to achieve study objectives and how it was determined, including all assumptions supporting the sample size calculation.

items.title: Recruitment
items.description: Strategies for achieving adequate participant enrolment to reach target sample size.

items.title: Allocation: sequence generation
items.description: Method of generating the allocation sequence; method of any restriction (e.g. blocking, stratification).

items.title: Allocation: concealment mechanism
items.description: Mechanism of implementing the allocation sequence describing any steps to conceal the sequence until interventions are assigned.

items.title: Allocation: implementation
items.description: Who will generate the allocation sequence, who will enrol participants, and who will assign participants to interventions.

items.title: Blinding (masking)
items.description: Who will be blinded after assignment to interventions and how; if blinding is to be broken, the circumstances under which unblinding is permissible and procedures.

items.title: Data collection methods
items.description: Plans for assessment and collection of outcome, baseline, and other trial data, including any related processes to promote data quality; reference to study instruments.

items.title: Data management
items.description: Plans for data entry, coding, security, and storage, including any related processes to promote data quality; reference to study protocols.

items.title: Statistical methods
items.description: Statistical methods for analysing primary and secondary outcomes; methods for any additional analyses; definition of analysis population; methods for handling missing data.

items.title: Data monitoring
items.description: Composition of data monitoring committee (DMC); summary of its role and reporting structure; statement of whether it is independent from sponsor and competing interests; reference to where further details about its charter can be found.

items.title: Harms
items.description: Plans for collecting, assessing, reporting, and managing solicited and spontaneously reported adverse events.

items.title: Auditing
items.description: Frequency and procedures for auditing trial conduct, if any, and whether the process is independent.

items.title: Research ethics approval
items.description: Plans for seeking research ethics committee/institutional review board approval.

items.title: Protocol amendments
items.description: Plans for communicating important protocol modifications to relevant parties.

items.title: Consent or assent
items.description: Who will obtain informed consent or assent from potential trial participants or authorised surrogates, and how.

items.title: Confidentiality
items.description: How personal information about participants will be collected, shared, and maintained to protect confidentiality before, during, and after the trial.

items.title: Declaration of interests
items.description: Financial and other competing interests for principal investigators for the overall trial and each study site.

items.title: Access to data
items.description: Statement of who will have access to the final trial dataset and disclosure of contractual agreements that limit such access for investigators.

items.title: Dissemination policy
items.description: Plans for investigators and sponsor to communicate trial results to participants, healthcare professionals, the public, and other relevant groups (e.g. trial registry results database, regulatory bodies).

items.title: Informed consent and biological specimens
items.description: Model consent form and other related documentation given to participants and authorised surrogates; plans for collection, laboratory evaluation, and storage of biological specimens for genetic or molecular analysis in the current trial and for future use in ancillary studies, if applicable.
